Chronic hepatitis B: Yellowing of the eyes or skin
Cerebrovascular disease: Sudden weakness on one side of the body
Coronary heart disease: Pain spreading to the jaw or left arm
Benign prostatic hyperplasia: Very weak or slow urine stream
Feeling like the bladder is not empty
Needing to urinate much more often at night
Cataract: Increased glare or halos around lights
Chronic low back pain: Numbness or tingling in the legs or feet
Sudden loss of bladder or bowel control
Non-alcoholic fatty liver disease: Yellowing of the eyes or skin
Pain in the upper right side of the belly
COPD: More mucus or phlegm than usual
Atrial fibrillation: Fainting or feeling like passing out
Dementia: Difficulty with daily tasks like dressing
Glaucoma: Nausea or vomiting with eye pain
Heart failure: Swelling in the ankles or legs
Waking up at night gasping for air
Asthma: Needing the rescue inhaler more often
Gout: Redness and warmth in a joint
Increased pain when touching a joint
Difficulty walking due to joint pain
Age-related macular degeneration: Straight lines looking wavy or distorted
Dark or empty spots in the center of vision
Depression: Loss of interest in usual activities
Feeling much more sad or hopeless
Sleeping much more or much less than usual
Feeling much more tired or low energy
Rheumatoid arthritis: Increased joint stiffness, especially in the morning
Joints feeling warmer to the touch
Parkinson's disease: More stiffness in the arms or legs
Increased difficulty walking or balance issues
More frequent 'freezing' when trying to move
Increased confusion or changes in thinking
Allergic rhinitis: More frequent sinus pressure or pain
Generalised anxiety disorder: Increased difficulty falling or staying asleep
More constant muscle tension or aches
Increased restlessness or inability to sit still
Chronic gastritis: Increased burning pain in the upper stomach
Feeling full very quickly after eating
